title: Key concepts: Hinduism and Buddhism
description: Check your knowledge on Hinduism and Buddhism in Indian culture!

assessment_items.question_content:
**Why did Buddhism appeal to people in lower castes?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: Wealthy Buddhists didn't have to donate a portion of their earnings.

choices.content: It required that wealthy Buddhists donate a larger portion of their earnings
it offered salvation for people regardless of social position
it made the caste system largely irrelevant and people in India stopped using it

assessment_items.question_content:
**Why might have King Ashoka promoted Buddhism?** 

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: A single religion might help foster national unity.

choices.content: he wanted to unify people in his kingdom around a single religion
he thought it was important the state be granted legitimacy through a popular religion
the doctrines of Buddhism may have given Ashoka a friendlier and more peaceful public image by association
he wanted to give people in his kingdom a sense of purpose

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which Indian religion asserted that your deeds in this life would determine the caste you were born into in a later life?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: Buddhism does not have the concept of karma, or the idea that your deeds in a past life would determine your position in this life.
